Title: Eclipse 2023-12 RC1 version 4.30 - java.io.FileNotFoundException: /css/dark.css
body:

Title: Disposing ValueBinding does not remove listeners placed on its model and target
body:
The code in ValueBinding that nullifies `target` and `model` conflicts with the later call to `super.dispose()`, preventing Binding (the superclass) from removing the "on dispose" listeners that it might have placed on those objects.

https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.ui/blob/8982941a20a264e7c06a9585006f35af1709f7b4/bundles/org.eclipse.core.databinding/src/org/eclipse/core/databinding/ValueBinding.java#L260-L262

https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.ui/blob/8982941a20a264e7c06a9585006f35af1709f7b4/bundles/org.eclipse.core.databinding/src/org/eclipse/core/databinding/Binding.java#L137-L147

The effect does _not_ seem to be deliberate, since:
* The change to ValueBinding was earlier, in commit e52e28d41bd4e6ac2b7d02d50fba457e69961880, while the dispose listener was not introduced to until a bit later, in commit 7978ebc16da1efae2a5bf089c64e76675b8b5b2e.
* ListBinding and SetBinding have a very similar `dispose` implementation, but they do _not_ nullify `target` and `model` themselves.
reasoning: Describes incorrect system behavior rather than a UX smell.

body:

Title: Enable code mining for method references by default
body:
Code minings in Eclipse are hard to discover for users, other IDEs enable (some) of them by default, hence making it obvious that they have it. In a recent workshop my clients were really surprised that the Eclipse IDE also has these.

I setting which I find useful and non-disruptive is the display of a method usage on top of methods.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/da78afea-e0c3-49b9-ab96-7786ec158aee)

Maybe we should enable this by default? This would signal to the user that we also support this.

cc @HannesWell @HeikoKlare @mickaelistria
reasoning: The UI gives weak clues for finding the feature.

Title: Content Assist wrong positioned on Ubuntu
body:
Hi There. I am using eclipse 2022-12 (4.26.0 Build id: 20221201-1913) on Ubuntu 22.04 with Java 17 and kernel 6.1.12-060112-generic.

When I call the content assist with CTRL+ SPACE then the window of the content assist get first correctly positioned but then after ~ 1s automatically wrongly re-positioned, somewhere at a weird position, often outside of the Eclipse Main Window. I assume it has something to do when the JavaDoc get in the background loaded and after that displayed next to the content assist.

Example how it looks like after it get automatically re-positioned

![Screenshot from 2023-03-04 16-31-26](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7382336/222914865-b75fc498-5c00-4dc8-9436-28328ff2468f.png)
reasoning: Visual or behavioral inconsistency not covered by narrower smells.

Title: [GTK] UI hang while using Find/Replace overlay
body:
Using `SDK-I20240728-1800-linux-gtk-x86_64` on RHEL 9.2 / `gtk3-3.24.31-2.el9.x86_64`

I had Find/Replace overlay open while starting Eclipse in debugger from IDE, and after returning to the debugger IDE froze.
Here is stack trace with frozen UI (no repaints anymore, java process hangs in native GTK code):


But got: The overlapping issue with code mining, already present in earlier versions, has worsened in 4.34. Upon closer inspection, it seems that code mining elements are reloaded/repainted twice instead of once, as in versions before 4.33.

This behavior also causes lines to shift, with new lines constantly being added and removed, making code mining unusable in larger projects!

*  Eclipse 4.35.0

Title: ShowViewMenu does not work for E4 Views
body:
E4 Views that are added to the Perspective over _org.eclipse.ui.IPageLayout.addShowViewShortcut(String)_ or similar mechanisms do not appear in the View Shortcut Menu. But are visible in the Show View > Other... Menu.

It looks like the _org.eclipse.ui.internal.ShowViewMenu.fillMenu(IMenuManager)_ method is only looking in the E3 View list for the view id: _WorkbenchPlugin.getDefault().getViewRegistry();_
Whereas the Dialog looks in the E4 Descriptor List: _((MApplication) element).getDescriptors();_
reasoning: Describes incorrect system behavior rather than a UX smell.

Title: Space key to select first working set doesn't work when "Select Working Set" dialog first appears
body:
When I import a project from git, I check the "Add project to working sets" checkbox, and I click the "Select" button to bring up the "Select Working Sets" dialog.  I do all of this from the keyboard.  At this point, I have two working sets, and both are unchecked in the dialog, and there is a focus indicator on the first one. I should now be able to press the spacebar to check the first working set.  What actually happens is nothing.  Pressing the spacebar at this point does nothing.

However, if I then press downarrow, and then uparrow, and then space, it checks the box.  I've verified this with 2022-12, but this has worked this way consistently the last several releases, at least.

Eclipse IDE for Java Developers (includes Incubating components)

Version: 2022-12 (4.26.0)
Build id: 20221201-1913
reasoning: Element appears actionable but does not respond usefully.

body:

Let's make sure issue is not already fixed in latest builds first.

- [x] I verified I can reproduce this issue against [latest Integration Build of Eclipse SDK](https://download.eclipse.org/eclipse/downloads/)

## Steps to reproduce

From a fresh installation and clean workspace:

Steps to reproduce:
* create several (a dozen) of longFileName_forALongTab[1..12].txt files
* Open them all
=> All editors are shown in tabs, so tabs are not visible and require to go on the "Show List" widget to be visible
* check the most left tab and "Show List": on most screens the few 1st tabs are not visible
* Select Last tab (on my screen it's the longFileName_forALongTab9.txt)
* Double-click on the editor tab to maximize it
EXPECTED => editor for longFileName_forALongTab9.txt maximized
GOT => editor for longFileName_forALongTab7.txt maximized

Analysis: it looks like this maximize action look at the index of the tab to identify which editor to select after maximize. However, as the editor area maximizes, more editors are visible, including some hidden because they too far on the left area. Those editors become visible on maximize so the index of the initially selected editor before maximize resolves to a different editor after maximize.

Title: [find/replace overlay] Separate UI from Controller Logic
body:
This Issue Documents a review-comment from the find/replace overlay PR
which could not be addressed into the current proposal
[comment](https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.ui/pull/1192#discussion_r1592696718) by @HeikoKlare 

```
I see some options for improving the design of this class, but I would leave this for follow-up work as this class is internal, so can be adapted easily, and I don't want to defer a merge of this functionality. Things I would consider to make the class more maintainable are at least:

- Separate the UI setup from the controller logic
- Maybe extract layouting functionality (which parts of the UI to show and where to place them) into a separate class
- Update UI elements via listeners on the FindReplaceLogicState rather than making interactions with one UI element change the enablement of another
```
reasoning: Describes incorrect system behavior rather than a UX smell.

Title: @AboutToHide method of dynamic menu contribution not called
body:
Since Eclipse 2022-06 The @AboutToHide annotated method of a dynamic menu contribution is longer called.

I'm pretty sure this is due to recent changes in https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/platform/eclipse.platform.ui/+/192363/3/bundles/org.eclipse.e4.ui.workbench.renderers.swt/src/org/eclipse/e4/ui/workbench/renderers/swt/MenuManagerHideProcessor.java
@jukzi An Eclipse 4 contribution always has an contribution object attached to it. For such an item the @AboutToHide annotated method won't be called. This leads to items kept in context menu when opening it again.

Fix made for Eclipse 2022-06: https://bugs.eclipse.org/bugs/show_bug.cgi?id=578964
reasoning: Describes an explicit error, bug, exception, or incorrect behavior.

Title: Accessibility: Navigating through Controls with Tab does autoscroll to the Viewer with Focus
body:
Let's make sure issue is not already fixed in latest builds first.

- [X] I verified I can reproduce this issue against [latest Integration Build of Eclipse SDK](https://download.eclipse.org/eclipse/downloads/)

## Steps to reproduce

From a fresh installation and clean workspace:
I created a Plugin Project.

I tried

1. Navigated to the Extensions Tab within the MANIFEST.
2. Added enough random extensions so that when making the window smaller, the Tree Viewer would have a scroll bar.
3. Set the Selection the last extension in the viewer.
4. Scroll up so that the selection is not visible anymore.
5. Navigating through each of the controls is possible using Tab or Shift+Tab. Navigate to the Filter Text Control above the Viewer (Shift+Tab for me). The Text Control should have gotten Focus.
6. Make the window as small as possible till you can't see the viewer anymore.
7. Click Tab

I expected: Focus is returned to the Viewer Control. There is autoscrolling so that the element in focus remains always in view. Since there are 2 scrollbars in this example (from the editor and from the viewer), both must be autoscrolled so that the focus control is in view, but also the selection within the control.

But got: Focus does get returned to the Viewer Control, but this is not visible until I scroll down to reveal the viewer and indeed it has focus.

Video: https://sap-my.sharepoint.com/:v:/p/guilherme_saraiva/EaHGXdtkUt5DktiwDpSvrx8B6iRTKbVw_OYc_lxLMhp8Tw?e=FjNILy&nav=eyJyZWZlcnJhbEluZm8iOnsicmVmZXJyYWxBcHAiOiJTdHJlYW1XZWJBcHAiLCJyZWZlcnJhbFZpZXciOiJTaGFyZURpYWxvZy1MaW5rIiwicmVmZXJyYWxBcHBQbGF0Zm9ybSI6IldlYiIsInJlZmVycmFsTW9kZSI6InZpZXcifX0%3D

## Why this is important

At SAP we have several use cases in which the Editor has a Tree Viewer with Selection Detail at the bottom, and one needs to scroll down to see it (simulated by making the window smaller above). Navigating with the Tab Key improves usability and contributes to an inclusive design, thus why it would be important that this feature works flawlessly.

**If you have any tips on where the related code could be found, I would very much appreciate it as a starting point for me as well**

## Community

- [X] I understand reporting an issue to this OSS project does not mandate anyone to fix it. Other contributors may consider the issue, or not, at their own convenience. The most efficient way to get it fixed is that I fix it myself and contribute it back as a good quality patch to the project.
reasoning: Contrast, visibility, or assistive technology support is poor.

Title: UI Freeze: deleting  large search Result
body:
When using the ctrl-h search feature, if I get a lot of search results (like 181,841 in my current example) if I highlight a project or folder which has a lot of entries with in it and press "delete" to remove those items from the current search results, it sends 1 of my cpu cores to 100% and the entire UI freezes. Even after 5 minutes the system remains frozen and the 1 cpu core is still at 100%.

I've had this happen 2 or 3 times now so it seems relatively reproducible.

os : fedora 38, xfce
jdk : openjdk 17.0.8+7
jvm memory : 4g
eclipse : version=2023-06 (4.28.0) build id: 20230608-1333
reasoning: Describes incorrect system behavior rather than a UX smell.

Title: A canceled search may keep looking for hits in a file.
body:
## The problem
When running a search, calls to `java.util.regex..Matcher.find(...)` might be very expensive. Currently, a search might make such calls even after being canceled because the monitor of the Job is only checked in steps of 20.

## Steps to reproduce
* Search for the following *regular expression* using the _Search_ dialog (`Ctrl + H` in Windows): `for \([\W\w]*\;*\)[\W\w\r]*new TreeItem`
* Cancel the search in the _Progress_ view
* Check the option "_Show sleeping and system operations_" in the preferences of the _Progress_ view

Result: you should see that some search operations are still running. They are marked as _(Cancel requested)_ though.

![image](https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.ui/assets/2205684/72e478fb-e6db-4984-9243-add983741ef7)

If you do a Thread Dump you will see that some worker Threads are still searching for hits, for example this one:

```
"Worker-283: File Search Worker" #21737 prio=5 os_prio=0 cpu=48718.75ms elapsed=261.44s tid=0x00000283db76b060 nid=0xd898 runnable  [0x000000f96d7fe000]
   java.lang.Thread.State: RUNNABLE
        at java.util.regex.Pattern.lambda$negate$7(java.base@17.0.6/Pattern.java:5626)
        at java.util.regex.Pattern$$Lambda$23/0x800000030.is(java.base@17.0.6/Unknown Source)
        at java.util.regex.Pattern.lambda$union$4(java.base@17.0.6/Pattern.java:5612)
        at java.util.regex.Pattern$$Lambda$284/0x0000000800ecbca8.is(java.base@17.0.6/Unknown Source)
        at java.util.regex.Pattern.lambda$union$4(java.base@17.0.6/Pattern.java:5612)
        at java.util.regex.Pattern$$Lambda$284/0x0000000800ecbca8.is(java.base@17.0.6/Unknown Source)
        at java.util.regex.Pattern$CharPropertyGreedy.match(java.base@17.0.6/Pattern.java:4281)
        at java.util.regex.Pattern$BmpCharProperty.match(java.base@17.0.6/Pattern.java:3955)
        at java.util.regex.Pattern$BmpCharPropertyGreedy.match(java.base@17.0.6/Pattern.java:4329)
        at java.util.regex.Pattern$CharPropertyGreedy.match(java.base@17.0.6/Pattern.java:4290)
        at java.util.regex.Pattern$SliceU.match(java.base@17.0.6/Pattern.java:4138)
        at java.util.regex.Pattern$StartS.match(java.base@17.0.6/Pattern.java:3641)
        at java.util.regex.Matcher.search(java.base@17.0.6/Matcher.java:1728)
        at java.util.regex.Matcher.find(java.base@17.0.6/Matcher.java:745)
        at org.eclipse.search.internal.core.text.TextSearchVisitor.locateMatches(TextSearchVisitor.java:476)
        at org.eclipse.search.internal.core.text.TextSearchVisitor$TextSearchJob.processFile(TextSearchVisitor.java:212)
        at org.eclipse.search.internal.core.text.TextSearchVisitor$TextSearchJob.run(TextSearchVisitor.java:173)
        at org.eclipse.core.internal.jobs.Worker.run(Worker.java:63)

```

## Expected behavior
The search should be canceled as soon as possible to avoid unnecessary calls to `java.util.regex..Matcher.find(...)`. 

## Tested under this environment:
* OS & version: Windows 10
* Eclipse IDE/Platform version (as shown in *Help > About*): 2024-06 (4.32),  I20240407-1800

Title: slow marker sort (many markers)
body:
Sometimes when there is a severe error (like missing target platform)  in the workspace i end up with many problem Markers which are sorted quiet slow:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/51790620/166679423-02172c56-b2f0-401f-bcc1-c31ed06d778b.png)

Looks like the slow thing is the language aware comparison.  I don't see much value of sorting problem message (usual english) by Locale aware Comparator. Even on german which has special sorting rules i would not need that feature. When i click on sort by description i just want to have same descriptions grouped.
Also i wonder if all those "performance optimizations " in MarkerSortUtil realy work as desired. It feels like the collatorcache is cleared too often. Also i wonder what that optimizations try to archive - getting the top(k) of n entries always has a minimal O(n+log(k)) which is not significant better than the trivial O(n*log(n)) when just sorting all entries.
reasoning: Describes an explicit error, bug, exception, or incorrect behavior.

Title: File Search considers text files without file extension as binary (regression from #2180 when CDT is installed)
body:
Let's make sure issue is not already fixed in latest builds first.

- [X] I verified I can reproduce this issue against [latest Integration Build of Eclipse SDK](https://download.eclipse.org/eclipse/downloads/) (tested with I20250527-1800)

## Steps to reproduce

From a fresh installation and clean workspace:

* Do **Help** > **Install new software**
* Paste "https://download.eclipse.org/tools/cdt/releases/12.0/" into the **Work with** field and type **Enter**
* Select **CDT Main Features** > **C/C++ Development Tools** and click **Next**, **Next**, **I accept...**, **Finish**, **Select All**, **Trust Selected**, and **Restart Now**

I tried

* Close the **Welcome** view
* Click **Create a project** under **Package Explorer**
* Select **General** > **Project** and click **Next**
* Type "dummyproject" as **Project name** and click **Finish**
* Do **File** > **New** > **File**
* Type "dummyfile" as **File name** and click **Finish**
* Type "foo" into the editor of "dummyfile" and do **File** > **Save**
* Close the "dummyfile" editor window
* Do **Search** > **File**, type "foo" into **Containing text** and click **Search**

I expected: "dummyfile" as single search result

But got: 0 matches in workspace

## Tested under this environment:
* OS & version: Windows 10 19045.5737
* Eclipse IDE/Platform version (as shown in *Help > About*): Version: 2025-06 (4.36), Build id: I20250527-1800

## Community

- [X] I understand reporting an issue to this OSS project does not mandate anyone to fix it. Other contributors may consider the issue, or not, at their own convenience. The most efficient way to get it fixed is that I fix it myself and contribute it back as a good quality patch to the project.

## Potential cause

I thin this is a regression caused by a combination of #2180 determining binary files by filename only, and eclipse-cdt/cdt#409 introducing a binary ELF File content type matching `[^.]+` file names.

I believe that the culprit is that the fix for #2180 does not consider the describer of binary file types, which in the ELF File case requires that the file starts with an ELF magic number:

https://github.com/eclipse-cdt/cdt/blob/2c82545ba0425ace706af241ee05fab6fb0080bc/core/org.eclipse.cdt.core/plugin.xml#L574-L593

I think `TextSearchVisitor.hasBinaryContentType` has to be extended to require that binary content types also match when considering the file content, maybe by also passing the content to `IContentTypeMatcher.findContentTypesFor` instead of just the file name.
reasoning: Technical, test, API, or maintenance issue unrelated to UX.

Title: Maven "pom.xml": popup for validation warning has wrong size
body:
I created a maven project from the archetype "org.wildfly.archetype:wildfly-jakartaee-webapp-archetype:35.0.1.Final", then I added three dependencies to pom.xml:

```xml
<dependency>
	<groupId>jakarta.servlet.jsp</groupId>
	<artifactId>jakarta.servlet.jsp-api</artifactId>
	<scope>provided</scope>
</dependency>

<dependency>
	<groupId>org.glassfish.web</groupId>
	<artifactId>jakarta.servlet.jsp.jstl</artifactId>
	<version>3.0.1</version>
	<scope>provided</scope>
</dependency>

<dependency>
	<groupId>jakarta.servlet.jsp.jstl</groupId>
	<artifactId>jakarta.servlet.jsp.jstl-api</artifactId>
	<version>3.0.1</version>
	<scope>provided</scope>
</dependency>
```

One dependency has a unnecessary "version" element. So a warning "Overriding managed version 3.0.2 for jakarta.servlet.jsp.jstl-api" is reported.
The popuop when hovering over the warning text has a wrong size.

When hovering over "version", the message is completely visible, but when resizing the popup, additional info about the "version" element becomes visible. Is this intended?
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/66030aa2-4da1-4cf3-a7d9-5d77063e3aba)

When hovering over the text content of "version", the message is clipped. A second warning is shown when resizing it:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bba76c27-e24f-49d9-8667-253664a42934)


Hope this the right place to report it. Some time ago I reported a similar "popup is too small" problem for the xml editor (https://www.github.com/eclipse-lsp4e/lsp4e/issues/214) and was told to report it here. But as I could not reproduce the issue, I did not do this ;-). Anyway, in the other issue it was suggested that it might be related to multiple warnings in one place, and the second warning could be such a case, as there are two messages.
reasoning: UI elements are clipped, cut off, or overlap.

Title: Search view doesn't update matches for Xtext DSL code
body:
Unfortunately I don't have a self containing example at hand, just saw the issue immediately by loking at the PR related to the Search view `PositionTracker` fix https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.ui/pull/3802.

I can see it with custom Xtext based application that provides various DSL (text file based) implementations.

1) Start Search -> File, make sure the filter matches some DSL files, like "Search for 'a'" text in any DSL file.
2) Search view shows those DSL files (OK), highlighting text matches (OK)
3) Double click on that file & remove the line with the match, save the file
4) Search view is supposed to update the result to show less matches.
5) For Xtext based DSL files this doesn't work - because Xtext by default doesn't use `ITextFileBufferManager`.

See `org.eclipse.search2.internal.ui.text.PositionTracker` constructor why this doesn't work:

https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.ui/blob/1c1d72e1d84af68bd77eb4db785c11494662b644/bundles/org.eclipse.search/newsearch/org/eclipse/search2/internal/ui/text/PositionTracker.java#L61-L64

Of course it would be nice if Xtext would use "common" text file buffers API, but it's obviously design decision which was made several years ago and changing that would be either not possible or breaking existing API/code.

One possible solution *on platform UI side* would be to add resource change listener in `PositionTracker`...

See also related issue / proposed workaround in LSP4E https://github.com/eclipse-lsp4e/lsp4e/pull/1501.
reasoning: Search returns results that do not match likely intent.

Title: setRedraw in Controls: implement a "performWithRedrawDisabled"-method
body:
A common pattern in inheritants of controls is disabling redraw, performing an action and re-enabling redraw.
This should not have to be done manually; doing this manually risks accidentally disabling redraw and never re-enabling it again, leaving the Control in a limbo-state.

Note: since some recursiveMethods might use setRedraw, the method keeps track of how often it was called. This means that calling `setRedraw(true)` n times and then `setRedraw(false)` n-1 times will leave the Control in a non-drawing state - for the rest of the session!

As implemented in #1191, a good solution is to implement a method that performs the actions for the implementor in a sandboxed way.
reasoning: Issue does not clearly concern user experience.

Title: Inconsistent highlighting on CTRL+H search
body:
## Steps to reproduce

From a fresh installation and clean workspace:

1. Open Eclipse PDT
2. Highlight a project and then type CTRL+H
3. Enter search text and hit "Search".
4. Highlight the project again and hit CTRL+H
5. You may or may not see the search text highlighted.

I tried

Posting the issue to Eclipse PDT at https://github.com/eclipse-pdt/pdt/issues/314#issuecomment-3305860851

I expected: to have it fixed.

But got: responses saying to report it here.

## Tested under this environment:
* OS & version: Linux Mint 22.2 Zara
* Eclipse IDE/Platform version (as shown in *Help > About*):

Version: 2025-09 (4.37.0)
Build id: 20250905-1456

The only thing to do as a workaround is look at the box and if it's not highlighted at the time, then hit CTRL+A to highlight it. It's annoying that I don't always catch it, then wind up with the new query prepended to the old query when I hit "Search".
reasoning: Visual or behavioral inconsistency not covered by narrower smells.

Title: LockManager.handleException errors on shutdown
body:
I've seen now two times sporadic `LockManager.handleException` errors logged on shutdown of the IDE.

Looks some of the recent changes in UI/SWT area caused that, bugzilla never saw that error before.
First one was reported with  4.24.0.I20220504-0230 build, but that doesn't mean this was introduced there, because error is random (only if some workspace task runs at shutdown time).

Looks like the Display is now disposed earlier on shutdown?

Could be side effect of removing synchronization block from Display.isDisposed() via https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.swt/issues/74 / https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.swt/pull/75

If you're not comfortable with this template for your particular report, feel free to erase it and write your report from scratch -->

From a fresh installation and clean workspace:
From the RAP / PDE IDE, after the welcome page, move the bottom layout tab group up a little bit and the PDE image disappears...

Default starting out:

```
   <extension

```

Open the Plug-In Image Browser

## Expected behaviour
I expect to see the current page highlighted in the bottom line which lists multiple page numbers.
reasoning: Technical, test, API, or maintenance issue unrelated to UX.

Title: Display's system colors are set to late for dark theme during workbench startup
body:
During the startup of an org.eclipse.ui.ide.workbench based application the system colors of the display class are set twice. The first time with the default colors and the second time with the current themes colors. The problem is that the second time is done after the parts are created. Therefore any parts which are using system colors of the display during their creation will have the wrong color. This especially affects all GEF3 based editors and with that all GMF, Sirius and Graphiti based editors.  

But it seems that also other parts in the Eclipse  Platform are using these colors. See for example Issue #259. 

@vogella worked on a first patch [1] which moved setting the theme colors earlier during startup. However on my setup these resulted in some NPEs. 

Please also see the original bug report on this: https://bugs.eclipse.org/bugs/show_bug.cgi?id=577912

[1] https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/platform/eclipse.platform.ui/+/189040
reasoning: Theme tokens, colors, or styles are inconsistent.

Title: Inconsistent of accessibility in wizard (Back, Finish buttons)
body:
Description of the issue:
•	In the Project Explorer select “Import…” from the context menu. 
•	In the tree select Plug-In Development->Features.
•	With the tab button set focus into the “Next” button. 
•	Press space button. You will go to the next page.
•	In this page you can see that the focus is in the “Back” button (grey rectangle) and the “Finish” button has a default sign (blue rectangle).  

![Eclipse Wizard 1](https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.ui/assets/137054346/b185dc05-8de7-4b36-b69b-79cb52c192f8)

This situation leads to the inconsistent of accessibility because the pressing “space” key on the keyboard leads to the back page and the pressing “enter” key on the keyboard leads to the finish.
But the space and enter keys should have the same functionality.  

Now I select another application, for example Word, and then go back to the eclipse. In the wizard the back button gets suddenly the default sign (blue rectangle). So, now the back button in the wizard has focus (grey rectangle) and default sign (blue rectangle).

![Eclipse Wizard 2](https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.ui/assets/137054346/8f14e147-b629-4f7f-a1b6-cf77186b8305)

My expectations are that the focus should be somewhere else, but not on the back button. Only one button can have the focus (grey rectangle) and default sign (blue rectangle). 

This issue does not happen when from the beginning for example the focus is on the input field.
reasoning: Contrast, visibility, or assistive technology support is poor.

Title: Find and Replace overlay blocks top content in editor
body: **Describe the bug** When using the Find and Replace feature in the application, the overlay appears in the top-right corner of the editor. This positioning causes the overlay to obscure the first and second lines of content, making it difficult or impossible for users to read or edit those lines without first closing the overlay. VS code - we can scroll to see the content when Find and replace overlay is still active/open **To Reproduce** **Expected behavior** The overlay should be repositioned or made draggable, or the editor should auto-scroll to avoid overlapping content. **Environment:** 1. Select the platform(s) on which the behavior is seen: - - [ ] All OS - - [x] Windows - - [ ] Linux - - [ ] macOS **Version since** Eclipse or SWT version since when the behavior is seen - latest build **Workaround (or) Additional context** Any workaround available ? [#2021 ](https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.ui/issues/2021)
reasoning: UI elements are clipped, cut off, or overlap.

Title: Tasks view can no longer be sorted by priority
body:
- [✓] I verified I can reproduce this issue against Eclipse SDK 4.33-M1  (Updated from prior versions)
   - Includes a few extras like Buildship
   - Running on JDK-17

## Steps to reproduce

From a clean workspace:

1. Create a Java Project and class `Test` with the following content:
        
        /**
         * TODO: Test
         * FIXME: Really
         */
        public class Test {
               // TODO: Here too
        	public static void main(String[] args) {
        	}
        }
2. Open the Tasks view
3. (If necessary) compile the code to populate the view

**Observations:**

* Click the Description or Line table headers to confirm sorting - **works**
* Click the Priority table header - **nothing happens**
* Try the [ ... ] menu > Sort By > Priority - **nothing happens**

**Comments:** This issue was also present in Eclipse 4.32, but I suspect it must have regressed relatively recently. I haven't used this view for some months, so I can't give a solid timeframe. Just that when do, the priority-sort is something I would always use (due to years of TODO tags from a whole team).

There is nothing relevant-looking in the log.

## Tested under this environment:
Eclipse SDK
Version: 2024-09 (4.33)
Build id: I20240704-1800
Windows 11

## Community
- [✓] I'd be happy to help try and pinpoint the regression (via git-bisect) if someone can give me some pointers on which repo/package/(ideally) class to focus on. And maybe answer a few questions if I get stuck trying to get a dev env set up in a way to do such a bisect.
reasoning: Describes incorrect system behavior rather than a UX smell.

Title: "Link with Editor" functionality not working with hierarchical project presentation
body:
When I click in an open Java editor then the project explorer does not jump to the current class.

Setup in Project explorer:
- Two working sets, each with one project.
- Project presentation=hierarchical
- Projects closed with "Collapse all"
- "Link with Editor" is activated

Tested on many versions of Eclipse. Latest tested Version: 2023-03 (4.27.0)
reasoning: Describes incorrect system behavior rather than a UX smell.

Title: Active view/part indicator consistency in light theme
body: When comparing the classic theme with the dark theme and the light theme it looks like the light theme is missing the blue indicator which highlights the currently active view/part. Every theme should visually highlight: - Which view tab is currently shown in a section - Which view tab is overall currently selected in the IDE (Where is the focus? Where can I currently use my keyboard?) Classic theme (Blue tab highlights currently selected view): Dark theme (Blue underline highlights currently selected view): Light theme (Tab gets rendered a little bit more grey but no blue underline indicator. Not consistent with dark theme.): I would suggest to align the dark/light theme and add the blue underline to the light theme. From an accessibility standpoint only using such a small contrast is questionable. When looking at other IDEs (IntelliJ, VS Code) they increase tab contrast + add an indicator. ## Tested under this environment: * OS & version: Mac Sonoma * Eclipse IDE/Platform version (as shown in *Help > About*): 20231201-2043
reasoning: Contrast, visibility, or assistive technology support is poor.

Title: Startup/shutdown performance: unnecessary processing of activity events
body:
## Issue
The activity management in `MutableActivityManager` and `ActivityPersistenceHelper` is inefficient when dealing with higher amounts of activities. Activity enablements are unnecessarily processed and persisted upon different events:
1. All activities enablements are persisted upon every activity enablement change in the preference store, even if only the enablement of an expression-controlled activity changes (which is not part of the persisted states):
https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.ui/blob/f4989c93f6e599d977cabf7b039836a771a3b035/bundles/org.eclipse.ui.workbench/Eclipse%20UI/org/eclipse/ui/internal/ActivityPersistanceHelper.java#L60-L62
2. Enablement changes of expression-controlled activities are processed (and persisted as stated in 1.) even if the workbench is already closing. This has no effect as the activity support is disposed anyway during workbench close. Since the activity management is disposed _after_ the service locator, which produces further events due to the evaluation service being removed, it receives and processes these additional events unnecessarily.

## Reproduction

This unnecessary processing probably has no noticable effect in many products, as the types and amounts of activities are not high enough to produce a perceivable performance impact. In larger products this can, however, have severe impacts. In our product the effect is an overhead during startup and shutdown of the workbench of multiple seconds.

I have created a _very_ artificial demo project with a large amount of defined activities and a large amount of enablement changes during startup/shutdown to make the impact obvious:

[ManyActivitiesPerformanceDemo.zip](https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.ui/files/12539744/ManyActivitiesPerformanceDemo.zip)

## Evaluation

I have executed that demo with the current state of implementation and compared it to improved implementations that address the mentioned issue (can be seen in PR #1085 and #1086).

On my machine, the results with the demo project across several runs were as follows:

Startup time:
Without unnecessary persistence: 350-500 ms
With unnecessary persistence: 1500-1700 ms

Shutdown time:
With early disposal: 350-450 ms
Without proper disposal: 1300-1450 ms

## How to proceed

I have started two PRs that address the mentioned issues (#1085, #1086). The changes of those PRs are the basis for the evaluation results mentioned above. In case you already see any side effects of addressing those issue that I am currently not aware of or, in general, reasons why the current behavior is required, I would be glad if you can let me know.
reasoning: Describes incorrect system behavior rather than a UX smell.

Title: SynchronizableDocument implementation is deadlock friendly
body:
Coming from https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform/issues/282.

The `SynchronizableDocument` is supposed to be "thread safe" version of `AbstractDocument` for use in both UI and background tasks, in contrast to `AbstractDocument`, which is not thread safe.

The code in `SynchronizableDocument` adds locks around few `AbstractDocument` methods which modify document data **and** notify listeners afterwards about changes.

This implementation is a parade example of what one should **NOT** do for the safe MT code. 
The anti-pattern implemented in `SynchronizableDocument` is:
 
1) Take a lock
2) Change the document
3) Notify a bunch of 3rd party code in listeners about document changes (!!!)
4) Release the lock

The problem here is that listeners can freely acquire any number of locks and trigger any async code during change event notification, **while holding the lock** on `SynchronizableDocument`.  Taking two or more locks in random order from different threads is the straight road to the deadlock. This is exact the problem we've faced in https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform/issues/282 and I believe I saw this problem with `SynchronizableDocument` in other places.

A general solution to fix this anti-pattern is to avoid dispatching events while holding a lock.
So the order should be

1) Take a lock
2) Change the document
3) Release the lock
4) Notify a bunch of 3rd party code in listeners about document changes

I have an experimental patch that implements this strategy. Let see how much it will break.
At least for https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform/issues/282 it would unblock the deadlock even without PDE fix https://github.com/eclipse-pde/eclipse.pde/pull/2186 (which is still needed to fix https://github.com/eclipse-pde/eclipse.pde/issues/431).
reasoning: Describes incorrect system behavior rather than a UX smell.

Title: Allow custom merge semantics for TextPresentation styles
body:
- [x] I verified [latest Integration Build of Eclipse SDK](https://download.eclipse.org/eclipse/downloads/) doesn't provide the feature.

## Suggestion

When implementing an ITextPresentationListener (in my case, a SemanticHighlightingReconcilerStrategy for LSP4E), it would be helpful to have more options than just merging or replacing a new set of StyleRange[]. 

Proposed implementation would be to slightly decouple `applyStyle(StyleRange, StyleRange)` (logic for combining the 2 styles) from the `applyStyleRanges` (logic for determining overlapping style ranges between existing style ranges and to-be-merged-or-replaced style ranges) 

More specifically, I would like to make it so that the Semantic Highlighting is able to replace some aspects of the existing TextPresentation, while merging others. For example:
- compare view highlight sets the background colour of a range that has been changed
- syntactic highlight from TM4E in LSP4E sets all apparent keywords for a language to bold
- semantic highlight (obtained at some point in the future from the language server) would like to unbold keywords that are actually not keywords, but preserve the background colour

(correct generic behaviour here is a bit tricky as LSP4E currently uses TM4E themes, which use SWT TextStyle to describe the desired change, without the capacity to distinguish between having and not having an opinion about bold. For this reason, it would be helpful to have a configurable option -> we can iteratively improve LSP4E to have a sensible coordination of semantic and syntactic highlighting without having to rewrite/hack TextPresentation functionality)
reasoning: UI elements are clipped, cut off, or overlap.

Title: Part with "allow multiple" created dynamically disappears after moving back to PartStack
body:
Hi,

with a few changes to a generated "Eclipse RCP application" project I can show the disappearing of parts.
The changes include the creation of a PartDescriptor for the generated view. The PartDescriptor allows 
multiple instances. The part is also removed from the PartStack in e4xmi.
In the generated Open-handler the part is dynamically created like this:
- find a PartStack
- if found
-- make it visible (in case the PartStack was minimized or is not visible after the last part closed)
-- create the part and add it as a child to the PartStack's children.
-- show the created part

This works as expected.

How a part disappears:
- moving a part from the PartStack to be next to the other parts of the PartStack
- moving the part back to the PartStack leads to one part (in the PartStack) being replaced with the moving part
- one part disappears

After digging around for a while I found that in StackDropAgent.dock(...) the part is removed
if the part's elementId is equal to a part's elementId in the PartStack (see variable "viewWithSameId").
Reading the comments in the code the intention was to remove Placeholder parts - if present.
As far as I see multiple instance of one part are not considered when searching for present Placeholder parts.

A fix could be to additionally test whether the part in the PartStack (variable "stackElement") is of type MPlaceholder and
only if it is a Placeholder instance _and_ the elementId's are equal it should be removed.

For this very small sample project I could test the described fix successfully. The parts can be moved around and back to the PartStack without disappearing.

What do you think?

Thanks.

OS: linux, Debian bookworm
Eclipse: 4.35
reasoning: The UI gives weak clues for finding the feature.

Title: Eclipse "Refreshing Workspace" forever, blocking any other actions
body:
I installed 2022-12 today, and I went through the same steps that I do for every release:
* Export preferences and mylyn task list from existing instance
* Install new version
* Launch new version
* Import preferences and task list
* Install plugins (spring, decompiler, emacs+, bash, MoreUnit, Spotbugs, Docker Tooling)
* Show Git Repositories View, which shows the same repositories as the previous version
* Start importing projects

When I start importing projects, Eclipse starts building projects, and this whole process takes quite a while, but it eventually completes.  I open the Progress View to watch it.

Now, with 2022-12, it appears to be "stuck" in this process, and I have to restart Eclipse to get it to resume. In fact, I can't even exit Eclipse normally. I have to kill the process.

Title: Large File Association with added editors and content types
body:
Eclipse Memory Analyzer handles huge heap dump files and generates giant index files.
It has content type / editor associations for those files.
It is an RCP application, and doesn't include the General>Editors>Large File Associations preference page.
It can be installed into the Eclipse IDE.
If the large file association 'not listed types' preference is turned on, then the end user would have to configure all the Memory Analyzer types to avoid a prompt from the large files associations.

1. Is there a way for the Eclipse Memory Analyzer feature to automatically turn on the file types / editors when it is installed / activated?
2. Eclipse Memory Analyzer operates on content types, not just file extensions. Some of the content types are provided to Eclipse Memory Analyzer by extensions, so Eclipse Memory Analyzer doesn't know at compile time the extensions. How should those extensions be provided?
3. How should files ending "*.hprof.gz" be handled? Memory Analyzer doesn't know in general how to handle gz files, so shouldn't register for large files for *.gz.
4. Should large file association be enhanced to work with content types?
reasoning: Technical, test, API, or maintenance issue unrelated to UX.

Title: Adding a child to a ParStack that is not currently visible creates a new one in the current perspective
body:
I want to add a `Part` to a `PartStack` that is currently not visible in a pure E4 application perspective.

This currently leads to a new part stack created in the _current_ perspective and the part not added to the _invisible_ one so if one later switch to that perspective the part is missing.

To reproduce one needs two perspectives (A+B) and a partstack X in B:

1. Make A the active perspective.
2. Have some code (e.g. a button) that creates a new `MPart` and search for X, e.g. `modelService.find("X", application);`
3. The add the new part to that stack `stack.getChildren().add(newPart);`
4. Now one can see a new Partstack created in `A` switching to `B` shows that `X` does **not** contain any new parts.
reasoning: The UI gives weak clues for finding the feature.

Title: Can't switch buffers by filtering for buffer names including the letter "B"
body:
This is very strange, so bear with me.

I'm using Eclipse IDE 2023-12 (4.30.0) on MacOS with Emacs key bindings. Switching windows/tabs/files is initiated with `Ctrl-X,b` and then you can immediately type into a filter to narrow things down e.g. `*.java` or `MyClass`, etc.

For some reason, the letter `b` does nothing in this filter.

If I have an open buffer with the name `Keyboard.java` and I type `Ctrl-X,b,Keyboard` then the filter will show the word `Keyoard` (note the missing `b`) which will match nothing. No amount of backspacing, etc. will get the `B` key to type into that filter.

But the `b` key isn't being *completely* ignored. If I press b in that dialog, it seems to scroll-through the buffer names, with each keypress selecting a different buffer from the list.

If I choose *Window > Navigation > Switch to Editor...* (which is labeled with the shortcut `^C ^B` (note that's `Ctrl-X,Ctrl-B` instead of a bare `B`), I get a different dialog that does roughly the same thing. There is no "filter" box to type into, but typing does indeed search around within the list of open buffers. And the `B` key works, at least to the extent that any key works there... the rules are quite murky in there.

Is there a reason I can't filter my buffers for the letter `b`? I haven't encountered any other key on the keyboard that doesn't work in this search filter box.
reasoning: Spacing, ordering, or alignment reduces clarity.

Title: Find/Replace overlay: Immediate Replace after searching selected text not working
body:
Let's make sure issue is not already fixed in latest builds first.

- [x] I verified I can reproduce this issue against [latest Integration Build of Eclipse SDK](https://download.eclipse.org/eclipse/downloads/)

## Steps to reproduce

When selecting a text in a file and opening the Find/Replace overlay the selected text is already entered into the search box.
If one subsequently opens the `Replace` part, types in a replacement and then hits Enter or presses the Replace button nothing happens.
I would expect that the previously selected text is replaced and if I continue pressing enter all other occurrences of that text.
If I press Search (or hit Enter in the Search text field) the next occurrence of the text is found and replace works as expected.
Therefore I assume replace is not working because the overlay is not yet in a 'Match-found' state.

In the Search/Find context it would probably be a bit odd to find the selected as first result, it would mean that one has to press Enter/Search twice to find the next occurrence. Therefore it is fine to start the search there after the selected text.
But in the context of replace I think it is reasonable to start the search before the selected text (or after if one replaces backwards, although that seems not to be possible).

Replace all is working as expected.

## Tested under this environment:
* OS & version: Windows 10
* Eclipse IDE/Platform version (as shown in *Help > About*): I20240629-1800

## Community

- [x] I understand reporting an issue to this OSS project does not mandate anyone to fix it. Other contributors may consider the issue, or not, at their own convenience. The most efficient way to get it fixed is that I fix it myself and contribute it back as a good quality patch to the project.

@Wittmaxi could you please have a look at this?
reasoning: Placement conflicts with user expectations.

Title: Filters contributed to org.eclipse.ui.ide.markerSupport overwrite each other
body:
The extension parsing mechanism for markerSupport.filters has a bug in merging contributed filters. I wrongly [assigns all extensions to the same array element](https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.ui/blob/master/bundles/org.eclipse.ui.ide/src/org/eclipse/ui/views/markers/internal/ContentGeneratorDescriptor.java#L150) because of not incrementing the index, thereby overwriting all except the last contributed filter. Title: StatusDialogManagerTest.testBug275867() flaky in Windows I-Builds
body:
The test case `org.eclipse.ui.tests.statushandlers.StatusDialogManagerTest.testBug275867()` sporadically fails in I-Builds on Windows:

```java
Dialog is not centered correctly expected:<Point {254, 102}> but was:<Point {254, 103}>

java.lang.AssertionError: Dialog is not centered correctly expected:<Point {254, 102}> but was:<Point {254, 103}>
at org.junit.Assert.fail(Assert.java:89)
at org.junit.Assert.failNotEquals(Assert.java:835)
at org.junit.Assert.assertEquals(Assert.java:120)
at org.eclipse.ui.tests.statushandlers.StatusDialogManagerTest.testBug275867(StatusDialogManagerTest.java:777)
at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:580)
```

From the values, it might be some rounding issue related to HiDPI support / monitor-specific scaling.
As an initial thought if we cannot reproduce the fail locally or have any other clue: we might add debug output with the actual and expected values to the test, such that we know how the values differ in case the test succeeds. It would be strange if in case of a success the values are the exact same as we see in case of a failure (except for the off-by-one), as there should be no non-determinism in how values are calculated/rounded. That's why I would expect the values to be completely different in case of a success, which could be verified by debug output.
reasoning: Visual or behavioral inconsistency not covered by narrower smells.

Title: Drag and Drop for Eclipse not Working Correctly in Gnome (Ubuntu 22.04)
body:
Since I've updated to Ubuntu 22.04 (with everything set to default, just the regular Gnome desktop), drag & drop in eclipse does not work as expected.

In the past, I could drag a `.java` file. If I drop it to the empty ground, I have two windows opened. Afterwards, I could move other `.java` files to the new window (by dragging and dropping them). Unfortunately, this does not work anymore, the second window does not react any longer (and dropping there results in the file going back to the original window). I can drop it to the empty space (creating a third eclipse window), but not to the existing window (which should produce two tabs in the same window).

This was working with Ubuntu 20.04 and is working with Fedora, so I assume that this is something caused by the update to 22.04. The issue exists both in eclipse 4.28.0 and 4.19.0, so I assume its caused by the window manager somehow.

This seems to be related to https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.ui/issues/677. But as far as I see it, #677 mainly concerns usability issues, while this is a problem making working with multiple windows basically impossible (with the Ubuntu version of Gnome, as it seems to me). 

Is there some configuration / workaround to fix this? (Probably, its only a Gnome configuration or some right thats missing. However, I could not find a fix for this so far).
reasoning: Describes incorrect system behavior rather than a UX smell.

Title: Provide missing Databinding methods for the generified variants of the now deleted non-generic variants
body:
We are in trouble over at the WindowBuilder project when the  `Databinding api deletion` #323 PR was committed. 

WindowBuilder is one of the most popular Eclipse plugins (consistently in the MarketPlace Top with close to 20.000 monthly downloads): [Link is here](https://marketplace.eclipse.org/metrics/successful_installs/last30days). People use WB to overcome the complexity of GUI design, especially regarding DataBinding.

The last time our project got in trouble with API deletion, it led to a revision of the deletion policy to delete API early (in M1), so we had time to catch and fix it. The catching part worked, but this deletion has no simple fix.

At this point, there is no drop-in replacement for the old API, which makes it practically impossible for us to fix this.

<details><summary>Take the following example</summary>

[Copied from here](https://github.com/eclipse/windowbuilder/pull/329#issuecomment-1278665861)

Replacing the Observables seems to be much more complicated. Take the following example

```
IObservableValue passObserveWidget = SWTObservables.observeText(passText, SWT.Modify);
IObservableValue passObserveValue = BeansObservables.observeValue(mySampleBean, "pass");
```

should become

```
ISWTObservableValue<String> passObserveWidget = WidgetProperties.text().observe(passText);
IObservableValue<Object> passObserveValue = BeanProperties.value("pass").observe(mySampleBean);
```

the problem is the split into required submethods. This does not seem to easily match the existing structure (as far as I understand it up to now). A rather easy approach would be to offer methods like `WidgetProperties.observeText(...)` such that it becomes a drop-in replacement.

</details>

At this point, there are three options: Revert this change, create a drop-in replacement for the old classes, or drop data-binding support from WB.

To keep WB alive, I request to revert the change.
reasoning: Placement conflicts with user expectations.

Title: [Feature Request] Option to keep toolbars inlined with view/editors tabs
body:
I work on a team that develops an application based on Eclipse RCP.  

As requested by our customers, we implemented some visual modifications regarding the way the view toolbar items behave when there is not enough space for them all to be drawn on the same line. 

Eclipse's default behavior is to move the toolbar and view menu to the bottom of the tab and add more lines to hold the items, as necessary. 

Here are some images of Eclipse's default behavior, using a screen from our application as an example: 

Window with space for all items: 
![img1](https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.ui/assets/113549558/48a6cc70-59d7-4d87-ad49-4c713f629822)

Window without enough space, creating a line below: 
![img2](https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.ui/assets/113549558/f3afcff0-5495-48d1-8529-2287a0bcbb30)

Window without enough space, creating more lines: 
![img3](https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.ui/assets/113549558/fba0c036-25b9-4117-bed5-31428b72e45b)


We implemented was an option for the toolbars to be kept in a single line, next to the tabs, as shown in the next images. 

Window without enough space. Therefore, each hidden item generates an entry in the “chevron” menu: 
![img4](https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.ui/assets/113549558/7efc2543-c1e7-4dc2-8abf-28d571a80f3e)

Window without enough space. In this case, all toolbar items went to the chevron menu. Note that the view menu also remained on the same line, next to Minimize button: 
![img5](https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.ui/assets/113549558/07717a64-7959-41a6-961a-1cdac004f54e)


Using the Eclipse 4 framework, we made a specialization of the `StackRenderer` class, responsible for managing the area called “top right” of the `CTabFolder`, where the view menu and the toolbar are located.  In this specialization, we reimplemented the `addTopRight` and `adjustTopRight` methods, changing the parent of the `ToolBar`, which is the toolbar component, to be a `CoolBar`, so to get the “chevron” behavior.  Other minor modifications were made to these methods and the `ToolBar` class. 

As a way to make Eclipse use this class, we created a Model Fragment with an Addon, which is responsible for overriding the "rendererFactoryUri", and, therefore, when any `MPartStack` element needs to be created, our class it is used. 

In several parts of this process, we need to access restricted parts of the Eclipse code. 

We think this setting could be useful on Eclipse platform itself.  Unfortunately, due to lack of time, we cannot contribute directly to the project ourselves.  If there is interest in implementing this feature, we're happy to share our code.
reasoning: The UI gives weak clues for finding the feature.
